description:
Emit SyncReport `created` entries for AutoCreate classifications:
GH issues with no stored number and no [bl-xxxx] tag in title. Entry
shape:
- title: issue title (will get [bl-xxxx] appended on the next push
  cycle, per B3 push half)
- description: issue body, sanitized via balls core's bl-b807
  sanitizer (which it already does at the display boundary; here we
  also bound the description size at intake — see size cap below)
- tags: GH issue labels mapped 1:1 to balls tags (subject to
  pathological-input guards)
- external.github_issues: {number, url, source:"github",
  synced_at:now}

WHY this is the security-sensitive child: SyncReport `created` writes
authoritative new tasks into balls core's task tree from
attacker-influenceable input (anyone with write access to GH issues
on the configured repo). bl-b807 sanitizer protects the *display*
boundary; bl-4673 ingest backstops protect against DoS (oversized
fields, creates-flood). This child must HONOR those backstops, not
re-derive them.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Per-issue body capped at the documented bl-4673 field-size limit;
  oversized body truncated-and-warned in add_note (not silently
  dropped — operator must see the truncation).
- Per-sync `created`-count capped at the documented bl-4673 batch
  limit; exceeding it warns and pages further creates to the next
  sync invocation (no silent drop).
- Labels mapping cap: pathological label set (1000+ labels) bounded
  to first 100, ordered by GH label position; warning logged.
- Tests for: simple auto-create, oversized body, oversized label set,
  creates-flood (500 new issues in one poll), label with control
  sequences (sanitized by bl-b807 path at display, but the ingest
  itself must not panic or drop).
- 100% coverage; under 300 lines per file.
title: Pull: created entries (auto-create balls task from new GH issue)
